commands.review_summary:
Updates the tools profile docs and Skill Workshop guide to list current `coding`/`messaging` tool visibility and add `skill_workshop` troubleshooting guidance for restrictive tool policies.

PR surface: Docs +22. Total +22 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. for this docs-only PR. I verified the documented behavior by source inspection of the current tool catalog and policy pipeline rather than by running a live Gateway.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the patch has no blocking findings and the remaining action is normal draft/maintainer review.

target.title: [codex] docs: clarify Skill Workshop tool visibility
target.body:
## Summary

- Update the tools profile table so `coding` and `messaging` reflect the current core tool catalog, including `skill_workshop` and `bundle-mcp`.
- Add Skill Workshop tool-visibility guidance for agents where stricter profile, provider/agent policy, or `tools.deny` hides the manual `skill_workshop` tool.

Refs #87570.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-catalog.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `npx oxfmt --check docs/gateway/config-tools.md docs/tools/skill-workshop.md`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm docs:list | rg '^(gateway/config-tools|tools/skill-workshop)\.md'`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: The public docs now describe the current `skill_workshop` tool visibility behavior: `tools.profile: "coding"` includes it, and stricter effective tool policies can still hide it unless the exact tool is allowed.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw docs checkout with the source docs and docs-list script.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `rg -n 'Tool visibility|skill_workshop|bundle-mcp|tools\.alsoAllow' docs/gateway/config-tools.md docs/tools/skill-workshop.md` after the docs edits.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the final docs grep:

```text
docs/gateway/config-tools.md:26:| `coding`    | `group:fs`, `group:runtime`, `group:web`, `group:sessions`, `group:memory`, `cron`, `get_goal`, `create_goal`, `update_goal`, `update_plan`, `skill_workshop`, `image`, `image_generate`, `music_generate`, `video_generate`, `bundle-mcp` |
docs/gateway/config-tools.md:27:| `messaging` | `group:messaging`, `sessions_list`, `sessions_history`, `sessions_send`, `session_status`, `bundle-mcp`                                                                                                                                    |
docs/gateway/config-tools.md:43:| `group:agents`     | `agents_list`, `get_goal`, `create_goal`, `update_goal`, `update_plan`, `skill_workshop`                                |
docs/tools/skill-workshop.md:174:### Tool visibility
docs/tools/skill-workshop.md:176:`tools.profile: "coding"` includes the built-in `skill_workshop` tool. If an
docs/tools/skill-workshop.md:187:    alsoAllow: ["skill_workshop"],
docs/tools/skill-workshop.md:193:is not required for `skill_workshop`.
docs/tools/skill-workshop.md:281:| `skill_workshop` is missing from agent tools   | Check the effective tool policy. `coding` includes `skill_workshop`; stricter profiles, provider or agent overrides, and `tools.deny` can hide it. Add `tools.alsoAllow: ["skill_workshop"]` or remove the deny rule for agents that need manual review. |
```

Observed result after fix: The edited docs contain the current `coding` allowlist entry for `skill_workshop`, the updated `group:agents` row, and a Skill Workshop troubleshooting row that points operators to effective tool policy and `tools.alsoAllow: ["skill_workshop"]`.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Gateway or publish the Mintlify site locally; this is a source-docs-only change.
target.author: sweetcornna
